claims.summary: Pharmacologic butyrate treatment attenuates expression of IL-1β, TNF, and IL-6 in microglia from aged mice after LPS challenge.
Clostridium butyricum treatment suppressed microglial activation in the brain of APP/PS1 Alzheimer's disease mice.
Sodium butyrate exacerbated neuroinflammation in an MPTP Parkinson mouse model, alongside worsening broader Parkinson-related pathology.
Sodium butyrate reduced neuroinflammation in 5XFAD mice and improved synaptic plasticity.
Sodium butyrate supplementation prevents ethanol-induced increases in pro-inflammatory cytokine expression and normalizes microglial changes in the hippocampus of mice.
Sodium butyrate reduces neuroinflammation in chronic alcohol-exposed mice by suppressing microglial activation and lowering pro-inflammatory cytokines.

claims.outcome: motor performance and dopaminergic neuron survival
claims.summary: Sodium butyrate improves motor performance and reduces dopaminergic neuron loss in MPTP-induced Parkinson's disease mice.
Sodium butyrate reduced infarct volume in a diabetic-stroke mouse model.
Sodium butyrate reduced prefrontal-cortex neuroinflammation in DSS ulcerative-colitis mice while also improving intestinal barrier integrity.
Sodium butyrate alleviated lead-induced neuroinflammation and improved cognition in a lead neurotoxicity context.
Sodium butyrate reduced M1 microglia-associated neuroinflammation in cardiac arrest mice.

claims.outcome: neurologic injury after traumatic brain injury
claims.summary: Short-chain fatty acid supplementation after traumatic brain injury attenuates neurologic injury via the gut-brain-microglia axis.

contradictions.hypothesis: The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: administration_route, disease_state, model_system, time_horizon. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_33785315::C1 may both be true under different contexts.

The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: administration_route, disease_state, microbiome_status, model_system, tissue. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_36555338::C1 may both be true under different contexts.

The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: administration_route, disease_state, model_system. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_36709599::C1 may both be true under different contexts.

The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: administration_route, barrier_status, disease_state, model_system, tissue. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_37665564::C1 may both be true under different contexts.
The sign flip may be explained by hidden moderators on axes: disease_state, model_system, tissue. Claim pmid_32556930::C1 and claim pmid_38340407::C1 may both be true under different contexts.

experiment_proposals.title: Test whether administration_route, model_system, disease_state moderates the effect of sodium butyrate on neuroinflammation
experiment_proposals.rationale: Claims pmid_32556930::C1 and pmid_33785315::C1 may only disagree because context axes differ: administration_route, model_system, disease_state. A factorial design can separate true sign flips from hidden moderators.
design: Run a factorial intervention varying administration_route, model_system, disease_state alongside sodium butyrate vs control.
Measure neuroinflammation and any mechanistic mediators tied to ['colonic inflammation', 'neuroinflammation'].
Include enough power to detect interaction effects, not just main effects.
predictions: If claim pmid_32556930::C1 is right, sodium butyrate should increase neuroinflammation in context A.
If claim pmid_33785315::C1 is right, sodium butyrate should decrease neuroinflammation in context B.
If the moderator hypothesis is right, the effect sign should depend on the manipulated context axis rather than stay globally fixed.
